Patch Set 12: Code-Review-1

Yes, the failures are due to this patch. Specifically, setting proc_dirs to all the possible proc_dirs rather than to just the proc dirs that exist causes the find command in test_133f to fail. I commented that this might be a problem in PS 11, but I wasn't sure, so I didn't touch it.  I'll fix it now by setting proc_dirs to only the existing ones.

Patch Set 14:

Emoly, thank you for resubmitting the test.  However, TEI-0000 isn't a real bug, nor is anything marked on the actual test failure.

While it seems unlikely that this patch could cause lustre-initialization-1 failures, blindly resubmitting patches for testing risks landing intermittent test failures, since they could just be retested until they pass.  Better to understand what the root problem is, and mark a failure there or see what can be done to move it along.

In this case it appears that LU-7215/TEI-4250 is the cause of the failure.

parents.subject: LU-7661 mgs: restrict MGS_SET_INFO to stripe parameters

commit.subject: LU-5030 tests: remove /proc paths from tests
commit.message:
LU-5030 tests: remove /proc paths from tests

Remove the hard-coded /proc paths from cancel_lru_locks().  This was
only kept around because of a legacy implementation of that function
where it listed all lru_size files and then clear'd them manually.

If the filenames contained '.' then "lctl {get_param,set_param}" get
unhappy, but if the "lctl {get_param,set_param}" built-in globbing is
used with a wildcard parameter like "MGC*" then the '.' in the param
does not get replaced by '/' during processing and it just works.

Fix sanity-sec.sh test_24 to use "lctl get_param -R".  I couldn't see
a functional difference between the two read checks in that test,
so only left a single check.
